name: Airline tickets
summary: Domestic and international flight tickets, all classes.

evidence.claim: US DOT requires airlines and ticket agents to display full fare including mandatory taxes and government fees in advertised prices.
Basic Economy fares from major US carriers frequently exclude carry-on bags, seat selection, and cancellation credit — making nominal ticket price misleading until ancillary fees are added.

criteria.name: total_price_all_in
criteria.notes: Total including baggage, seat, meal, cancellation for user's actual trip profile. DOT requires taxes/government fees included in advertised price (E1) but does NOT require airline-imposed ancillaries.

criteria.name: schedule_reliability
criteria.notes: On-time performance by route and carrier; DOT publishes monthly.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Basic Economy headline fare; actual trip cost with carry-on ($50+), seat selection ($30+), and cancellation flexibility is often 2-3× the headline (E2).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the cited fare Basic Economy? What are the ancillary charges for the user's actual needs?

confabulationPatterns.reality: 'Non-stop' means no intermediate stops; 'direct' can mean the same flight number but with intermediate stops. These are not synonyms.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is 'direct' being used to imply 'non-stop'? Check the stop count.

counterfeitSignals.signal: Third-party ticket seller with price >30% below carrier's direct price
counterfeitSignals.action: flag as potential scam or non-refundable wholesale arrangement

compatibilityQuestions: Do you need a carry-on? Basic Economy typically disallows.
Would you change plans if needed? Basic Economy typically has no credit; Main Cabin has reuse credit; refundable has full refund.
Checking elite status? Some fare classes don't earn miles or status.
